checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2016 comparative balance sheet / income statement

evidence.quote: AR/Revenue ratio rose from ~11.4% (2011: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=50,159,530.24 / Revenues=440,385,997.23) to ~14.5% (2015: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=101,439,692.72 / Revenues=698,074,839.24) before reverting to ~12.6% in FY2016 (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=104,291,647.53 / Revenues=825,637,818.04)

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2016 cash flow statement / income statement

evidence.quote: OCF/NI coverage declined from ~3.2x in FY2013 (NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=67,198,410.26 vs NetIncomeLoss=21,129,265.21) to ~1.2x in FY2015 (34,537,327.77 vs 28,842,718.00) to negative in FY2016

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold data is only provided through 2011-03-31 (value=611,465.31, quarterly) and 2010-07-01/2011-06-30 (annual value=2,741,131.57); no COGS figures are provided for FY2012 through FY2016
evidence.location: 10-K FY2011 income statement; absent in later filings

evidence.quote: InventoryNet remains small relative to Revenues throughout (e.g., InventoryNet=2,460,586.01 vs Revenues=825,637,818.04 for FY2016), making inventory-based margin inference unreliable without COGS data

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: OperatingIncomeLoss=40,459,008.94 (FY2015-07/2016-06, annual) remained strongly positive while NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=-4,038,461.01 for the same period, and quarterly OCF swung to -17,675,144.94 (2015-07-01 to 2015-09-30) even as quarterly NetIncomeLoss remained positive at 8,809,285.42 for the same quarter
evidence.location: 10-K FY2016 income statement and cash flow statement

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: Allowance/AR ratio fell from ~1.61% in 2011 (AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=809,862.17 / AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=50,159,530.24) to ~0.77% in 2015 (776,537.70 / 101,439,692.72) while AR nearly doubled, then jumped back to ~1.18% in 2016 (1,230,680.50 / 104,291,647.53)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filed 2015-03-30, only about six months after the original 10-K was filed 2014-09-15

Two 10-Q/A amendments filed on the same day, 2012-05-25 (accession 0001008886-12-000085 for period 2011-09-30 and 0001008886-12-000086 for period 2011-12-31), indicating simultaneous restatement of two prior quarters
Recurring SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence entries across multiple years (2005, 2009, 2011, 2013, 2016) indicate repeated SEC comment-letter review cycles
evidence.location: Filing chronology list, various UPLOAD/CORRESP entries

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Balance sheet and cash flow data are available on a fairly continuous quarterly/annual basis from 2011-06-30 through 2016-06-30, but CostOfGoodsAndServicesSold is only populated through 2011-03-31, and the total 'Liabilities' tag has only four scattered data points (2011-06-30, 2012-03-31, 2012-06-30, 2013-03-31), limiting margin and full liability-structure analysis

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Premature or aggressive revenue recognition (e.g., extended credit terms, channel stuffing, or bill-and-hold arrangements) that inflated recorded revenue and receivables during FY2013-FY2015, which subsequently could not be converted to cash, producing a sharp operating cash flow shortfall (turning negative) in FY2016 even as reported net income stayed positive.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent grew +36.5% (60,156,871.83 to 82,123,898.77) FY2013->FY2014 vs Revenues growth of +23.7% (475,569,664.76 to 588,456,701.10)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Under-provisioning for doubtful accounts during the high-growth years (allowance coverage ratio fell from 1.61% to 0.77% of gross receivables from 2011 to 2015), which understated bad debt expense and overstated net receivables/net income, followed by a catch-up increase in the allowance (to 1.18%) in FY2016 coincident with the AR growth deceleration and cash flow shortfall.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent/AccountsReceivableNetCurrent fell from 1.61% (2011-06-30: 809,862.17/50,159,530.24) to 0.77% (2015-06-30: 776,537.70/101,439,692.72), then rose to 1.18% (2016-06-30: 1,230,680.50/104,291,647.53)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible capitalization of costs that should have been expensed (reflected in the near-4x jump in OtherAssetsNoncurrent in FY2016 and large stepped increases in Goodwill from acquisitions), inflating non-current assets and understating operating expenses, thereby overstating net income relative to the underlying cash-generating capacity of the business.

rationale_evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=8,879,809.30 (2015-06-30) -> 33,589,517.79 (2016-06-30), +278%, while Revenues grew only 18.3% over the same period

top_signals: Receivables grew materially faster than revenue for three consecutive fiscal years (FY2013-FY2015), then abruptly decelerated in FY2016
Operating cash flow turned negative (-$4.0M, FY2016) while net income remained positive ($19.3M), reversing a multi-year trend of OCF exceeding NI
Allowance for doubtful accounts as a percentage of gross receivables declined from 1.61% to 0.77% during the peak growth years before snapping back to 1.18% in FY2016
Non-current 'soft' assets (Goodwill, OtherAssetsNoncurrent) grew far faster than revenue, including a ~278% single-year jump in OtherAssetsNoncurrent in FY2016
Filing history shows a 10-K/A restatement shortly after the original 10-K and simultaneous restatement of two prior 10-Qs, alongside recurring SEC correspondence
